engineGuide.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Bentley Flying Spur across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Early 6.0L W12 twin-turbo powering first-gen Flying Spur; robust but timing chain tensioner requires attention.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur, Flying Spur Mulliner, Flying Spur Azure Edition
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - hydraulic unit degradation common at 70-90k miles per Bentley TSB 2011-04.
Turbo oil seal leakage - Garrett turbo cartridge seals wear causing oil consumption at 70k+ miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for BWB Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Refined W12 with updated cooling and turbo systems; plastic coolant pipes require proactive replacement.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur, Flying Spur V8 S, Flying Spur Mulliner, Flying Spur Speed (early)
commonFailures: Plastic coolant pipe failure - cracks at 80-100k miles causing catastrophic overheating; aluminium upgrade standard.
Timing chain tensioner wear - revised hydraulic design but still requires monitoring at high mileage.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCHB Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: Latest W12 with emissions refinements and ULEZ compliance documentation; most advanced Flying Spur W12.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur (third generation), Flying Spur Mulliner, Flying Spur First Edition
commonFailures: Turbo oil seal wear - ceramic-coated cartridges recommended in rebuilds for extended longevity.
Direct injection carbon buildup - walnut blasting intake valves included in specialist rebuild packages.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CCHA Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 6.0L Petrol
entries.history: High-output 650bhp W12 for Speed variants; performance rebuilds include forged internals and custom mapping.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur Speed, Flying Spur Speed Mulliner
commonFailures: High-stress component fatigue - forged pistons and uprated turbos recommended for performance rebuilds.
Turbo oil seal wear - elevated boost pressures accelerate seal degradation at 60-80k miles.
entries.cta: Get quotes for BHT Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: EA888-derived 4.0L twin-turbo V8; highest-volume Flying Spur engine with timing chain upgrades standard.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur V8, Flying Spur V8 Mulliner
commonFailures: Timing chain guide wear - composite guide upgrades per VW Group TSBs essential at 70-90k miles.
Turbo actuator failure - wastegate actuators causing boost errors; ODIS calibration required post-replacement.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTGA Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Mid-cycle refined V8 with updated turbo actuators and calibration support; improved reliability over CTGA.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur V8 (late second gen), Flying Spur V8 S
commonFailures: Turbo actuator wear - revised actuators included in rebuilds with ODIS verification.
Camshaft adjuster solenoid failure - variable timing errors from worn solenoids; replacement with adaptation standard.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTGB Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 4.0L Petrol
entries.history: Performance-tuned V8 S variant with sport mapping and uprated intercoolers; specialist rebuilds retain performance character.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur V8 S, Flying Spur V8 S Mulliner
commonFailures: Turbo actuator failure - performance mapping increases actuator load; upgraded units recommended.
Oil consumption at high mileage - updated piston rings and PCV valve per TSB for rebuilds.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CTGC Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol Hybrid
entries.history: 3.0L V6 PHEV combining petrol and electric power; requires IMI Level 4 certified technicians for safe rebuilds.
compatibleVariants: Flying Spur Hybrid, Flying Spur Hybrid Mulliner
commonFailures: Hybrid battery degradation - capacity loss at 80-100k miles; module testing and replacement options available.
High-voltage cooling system complexity - specialist rebuilds include cooling circuit inspection and component replacement.
entries.cta: Get quotes for CHA Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Bentley Flying Spur's W12 and V8 twin-turbo engines deliver exceptional performance but face known challenges including timing chain tensioner wear, coolant pipe failures, and turbo oil seal degradation. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the problem groups below as a decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific variant.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Bentley Flying Spur W12 & V8 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner degradation and composite guide wear cause chain stretch, leading to rattling noises and potential catastrophic interference if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace hydraulic tensioner and guides before failure; chain inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch; prevents major damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full timing chain, tensioners, guides and seals replacement; engine removal required
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life 40-60k miles if no secondary damage; labour-intensive

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioners, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage vehicles; eliminates recurrence risk with upgraded components

problems.recommendation: On a Bentley Flying Spur worth £40,000-£80,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles offers exceptional value. If rattling is present above 80k miles, full replacement with upgraded components is typically more cost-effective than intermediate repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt CTGA/CCHB engine prices for your Bentley Flying Spur

problems.h4: Coolant Pipe Crack Failure - Why Bentley Flying Spur W12 Engines Overheat
problems.rootCause: Plastic coolant pipe material degrades under heat cycling, developing hairline cracks that cause sudden coolant loss and catastrophic overheating.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cracked plastic pipe with aluminium upgrade; coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents recurrence if caught before overheating damage; quick turnaround

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Cylinder head inspection, gasket replacement, warped component machining
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if overheating was brief; risk of hidden damage remains

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt W12 with aluminium coolant pipes standard, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if overheating warning appeared; eliminates hidden damage risk

problems.recommendation: If your CCHB W12 shows any coolant loss or temperature fluctuation, act immediately. Aluminium pipe upgrades cost under £1,300 fitted and prevent £20k+ engine damage. If overheating occurred, rebuilt replacement is the only safe option.
problems.cta: Get quotes for CCHB Bentley Flying Spur W12 replacement engines

problems.h4: Turbo Oil Seal Failure - Why Bentley Flying Spur Engines Consume Oil
problems.rootCause: Garrett turbocharger oil seals degrade under high heat and boost pressure, allowing oil to enter intake/exhaust causing consumption, smoke, and potential catalytic damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbos, replace seals and feed lines; ceramic-coated cartridges optional
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for early-stage wear; requires specialist turbo expertise

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace complete turbo cartridges with revised seals and bearings; calibration included
repairOptions.longevity: Extends life 50-70k miles; recommended for performance variants

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with ceramic-coated turbos and revised feed lines standard
repairOptions.longevity: Best for high-mileage vehicles; eliminates recurrence with upgraded turbo components

problems.recommendation: For oil consumption under 1L/1,000 miles, turbo seal service offers good value. If smoke is visible or consumption exceeds 1.5L/1,000 miles, rebuilt engine replacement with upgraded turbos provides better long-term value and warranty protection.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt turbo-equipped Bentley Flying Spur engine prices

problems.h4: Hybrid Battery Degradation - Why Bentley Flying Spur PHEV Range Declines
problems.rootCause: High-voltage battery modules lose capacity through charge cycling; cooling system complexity accelerates degradation if maintenance intervals are missed.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: IMI Level 4 diagnostic, module voltage testing, cooling circuit inspection
repairOptions.longevity: Identifies degradation early; informs replacement vs repair decision

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace degraded HV modules, recalibrate BMS, cooling system service
repairOptions.longevity: Restores 70-85% original range; viable if petrol engine is healthy

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt CHA powertrain with battery health certification, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for high-mileage hybrids; ensures integrated system reliability

problems.recommendation: Hybrid Flying Spur owners should schedule battery health checks at 60k miles. If range has dropped below 10 miles electric-only, module replacement offers good value. For vehicles over 100k miles, full rebuilt powertrain replacement provides comprehensive warranty coverage.
problems.cta: Enquire about CHA Bentley Flying Spur Hybrid engine availability

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Bentley Flying Spur
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain wear, coolant pipe cracks, turbo seal degradation, and hybrid battery decline are the most common failure patterns affecting Bentley Flying Spur engines in the UK. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Bentley Flying Spur specialists who supply rebuilt engines with upgraded components addressing these exact failure points. Every engine includes a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options. Compare prices today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Bentley Flying Spur Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Bentley Flying Spur and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Bentley Flying Spur is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the correct replacement.

groups.title: Bentley Flying Spur Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Bentley Flying Spur 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0L twin-turbo V8 powers most second and third-generation Flying Spurs, offering the best value replacement option with EA888-derived architecture and widely available cores. Introduced in 2012, this displacement balances performance and efficiency, making it the highest-volume engine for Flying Spur replacements.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain guide wear at 70-90k miles; turbo actuator failure causing boost errors
items.cta: Compare Bentley Flying Spur 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Bentley Flying Spur 6.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 6.0L W12 twin-turbo defines the Flying Spur's ultra-luxury character, with complex bank angles requiring specialist rebuild expertise. Spanning all three generations, this displacement commands premium pricing but delivers exceptional smoothness and low-end torque.
commonFailurePoints: Plastic coolant pipe cracks (CCHB) at 80-100k miles; timing chain tensioner wear; turbo oil seal degradation
items.cta: Get quotes for Bentley Flying Spur 6.0L petrol engines

items.title: Bentley Flying Spur 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0L V6 forms the petrol component of the Flying Spur Hybrid PHEV system, paired with an electric motor for combined 449hp output. Specialist rebuilds require IMI Level 4 certification due to high-voltage system integration and complex cooling requirements.
commonFailurePoints: Hybrid battery degradation at 80-100k miles; high-voltage cooling system complexity
items.cta: Enquire about Bentley Flying Spur 3.0L hybrid engine availability

fuelTypes.tag: Bentley Flying Spur Engines Fuel Type
fuelTypes.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engines by Fuel Type
fuelTypes.intro: Choose your Bentley Flying Spur engine by fuel type to compare realistic replacement prices, known issues, and compatible UK supply options - from efficient V8 to flagship W12.

items.title: Bentley Flying Spur Petrol Engines
items.description: All Bentley Flying Spur engines are petrol-only, with twin-turbo V8 and W12 configurations dominating UK replacement enquiries across three generations.
items.descriptor: All Bentley Flying Spur engines are petrol-only, with twin-turbo V8 and W12 configurations dominating UK replacement enquiries across three generations.
items.familiesLabel: Common Bentley Flying Spur Petrol Engine Families
families: CTGA/CTGB/CTGC - 4.0L V8 twin-turbo, highest-volume replacement engine with timing chain upgrades standard
CCHB/CCHA - 6.0L W12 twin-turbo, flagship displacement requiring specialist rebuild expertise
BWB/BHT - Early and high-output W12 variants, heritage cores with decreasing availability
foundIn: Second Generation (2013-2019) - CCHB W12, CTGA/CTGB/CTGC V8
Third Generation (2019-present) - CCHA W12, CTGA/CTGC V8
knownFor: Exceptional low-end torque and refinement from twin-turbo configurations
Timing chain tensioner wear on W12 and V8 at 70-90k miles requiring proactive attention
High UK replacement demand on V8 variants due to production volumes and known failure patterns
items.cta: Compare Bentley Flying Spur Petrol engine prices

items.title: Bentley Flying Spur Hybrid / Electrified Engines
items.description: The Flying Spur Hybrid combines a 3.0L V6 petrol engine with electric motor assistance, requiring certified high-voltage technicians for safe rebuilds.
items.descriptor: The Flying Spur Hybrid combines a 3.0L V6 petrol engine with electric motor assistance, requiring certified high-voltage technicians for safe rebuilds.
items.familiesLabel: Bentley Flying Spur PHEV Engine Codes
families: CHA - 3.0L V6 PHEV, complex cooling and high-voltage system integration
3.0L V6 petrol engine paired with electric motor for 449hp combined output
Regenerative braking recovers energy to recharge high-voltage battery pack
Replacement requires IMI Level 4 certification and high-voltage isolation testing
importantNotes: Hybrid rebuilds require specialist high-voltage certification; limited supplier availability
Battery health assessment recommended during engine replacement for comprehensive coverage
ULEZ compliance documentation available for post-2015 hybrid variants
items.cta: Enquire about Bentley Flying Spur Hybrid engine availability

engineYears.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Bentley Flying Spur's production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Flying Spur launched with the 6.0L W12 BWB engine, establishing the model's ultra-luxury credentials.
keyChanges: Launch of first-generation Flying Spur based on Continental GT platform
Exclusive 6.0L W12 twin-turbo engine (BWB code) across all variants
Introduction of Mulliner and Azure Edition bespoke trims
mainEngines: BWB - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (Flying Spur, Mulliner, Azure)
knownFor: Exceptional refinement and low-end torque from complex W12 configuration
Early timing chain tensioner wear emerging at 70-90k miles per Bentley TSB 2011-04
Decreasing core availability for BWB engines; specialist heritage documentation recommended
years.cta: Get quotes for 2005-2012 Bentley Flying Spur replacement engines
years.ticker: Bentley Flying Spur BWB engine replacement • Bentley Flying Spur W12 timing chain fix • Bentley Flying Spur first gen recon engine
enquiries: Bentley Flying Spur BWB engine replacement
Bentley Flying Spur W12 timing chain fix
Bentley Flying Spur first gen recon engine

years.description: The refined second generation introduced the 4.0L V8 alongside updated W12, creating the highest-volume replacement period for Flying Spur engines.
keyChanges: Introduction of 4.0L twin-turbo V8 (CTGA) alongside refined W12 (CCHB)
V8 S performance variant launched with CTGC code and sport mapping
Hybrid PHEV variant introduced with CHA code (2014)
mainEngines: CCHB - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (Flying Spur W12, Mulliner)
CTGA/CTGB/CTGC - 4.0L petrol V8 twin-turbo (Flying Spur V8, V8 S)
CHA - 3.0L petrol V6 PHEV hybrid (Flying Spur Hybrid)
knownFor: Best balance of reliability and value on V8 variants; competitive rebuild pricing from £11,200
Plastic coolant pipe failure on CCHB W12 at 80-100k miles requiring aluminium upgrades
Timing chain guide wear on EA888-derived V8 engines; composite upgrades standard in rebuilds
years.cta: Get quotes for 2013-2019 Bentley Flying Spur replacement engines
years.ticker: Bentley Flying Spur CTGA timing chain fix • Bentley Flying Spur CCHB coolant pipe replacement • Bentley Flying Spur V8 vs W12 cost
enquiries: Bentley Flying Spur CTGA timing chain fix
Bentley Flying Spur CCHB coolant pipe replacement
Bentley Flying Spur V8 vs W12 cost

years.description: The latest generation features updated W12 (CCHA) and V8 (CTGA/CTGC) engines with emissions refinements and ULEZ compliance documentation.
keyChanges: Updated CCHA W12 with emissions compliance refinements and ULEZ certification
Refined CTGA/CTGC V8 with improved turbo actuators and calibration support
Hybrid refresh (2021) with updated CHA system and cooling enhancements
mainEngines: CCHA - 6.0L petrol W12 twin-turbo (latest Flying Spur W12)
CTGA/CTGC - 4.0L petrol V8 twin-turbo (latest Flying Spur V8)
CHA - 3.0L petrol V6 PHEV hybrid (updated Flying Spur Hybrid)
knownFor: Latest emissions compliance documentation available for ULEZ and MoT readiness
Advanced ODIS coding support required for immobiliser pairing on replacement engines
Limited core availability for latest-generation engines; advance ordering recommended
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019+ Bentley Flying Spur replacement engines

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Bentley Flying Spur was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Bentley Flying Spur specialists.

faq.h2: Bentley Flying Spur Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement costs range from £11,200 for V8 rebuilt units to £24,900+ for W12 Speed variants, depending on engine code, variant and rebuild specification. All prices are supply-only; supply & fit adds £1,800-£3,500.
keyPoints: V8 CTGA rebuilt engines from £11,200-£14,800 supply only
W12 CCHB/CCHA rebuilt engines from £18,500-£25,600 supply only
Hybrid CHA powertrains from £16,400-£21,000 due to specialist certification
All rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists
items.cta: Compare Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement prices for your variant

items.question: What engine codes are used in the Bentley Flying Spur?
items.answer: Flying Spur engine codes include BWB/CCHB/CCHA/BHT for W12 variants, CTGA/CTGB/CTGC for V8 models, and CHA for Hybrid PHEV. Verify your exact code via VIN before ordering to avoid costly fitment errors.
keyPoints: W12 codes: BWB (2005-2013), CCHB (2013-2019), CCHA (2019+), BHT (Speed)
V8 codes: CTGA (base), CTGB (mid-cycle), CTGC (V8 S performance)
Hybrid code: CHA (3.0L V6 PHEV system)
Enter registration above for instant engine code verification before ordering
items.cta: Get quotes for your specific Bentley Flying Spur engine code

items.question: How long does a Bentley Flying Spur engine replacement take?
items.answer: V8 rebuilt engines typically dispatch in 4-6 working days; W12 and Hybrid require 7-10 days due to specialist rebuild complexity. Supply & fit installation adds 2-3 days for V8, 3-5 days for W12/Hybrid.
keyPoints: V8 CTGA units most readily available; W12 cores may require advance notice
Specialist fitting includes ODIS immobiliser coding and calibration verification
Nationwide delivery available; flexible scheduling to minimise vehicle downtime
All suppliers provide tracking updates from order to dispatch
items.cta: Book Bentley Flying Spur engine supply & fit with guaranteed turnaround

items.question: Do I need ECU coding after fitting a new Bentley Flying Spur engine?
items.answer: Yes, all Flying Spur replacement engines require ODIS immobiliser coding and adaptation support to pair with your vehicle's ECU. Our specialist network includes referral partners for this critical post-install step.
keyPoints: ODIS coding essential for immobiliser pairing and ECU adaptation
W12 and Hybrid variants require Bentley-specialist diagnostic capability
Coding support included in supply & fit packages; standalone referrals available
Failure to code correctly can result in "bricked" ECU and no-start conditions
items.cta: Get quotes including ODIS coding support for your Bentley Flying Spur

items.question: What warranty comes with a Bentley Flying Spur replacement engine?
items.answer: All rebuilt and reconditioned Bentley Flying Spur engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering internal components. Warranty excludes ancillaries, improper installation, and non-OEM modifications.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on all rebuilt units
Coverage includes internal components: pistons, bearings, timing system, turbos
Exclusions: ancillaries (alternator, starter), improper installation, non-OEM parts
Always confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Bentley Flying Spur engine warranty terms from UK specialists

items.question: Can I upgrade my Bentley Flying Spur engine during replacement?
items.answer: Yes, performance rebuilds are available for Flying Spur engines including forged W12 internals, uprated V8 turbos, and custom ECU mapping. Request quotes specifying enhanced specifications for your variant.
keyPoints: Forged pistons and uprated turbos available for W12 Speed and V8 S variants
Custom ECU mapping packages enhance power while maintaining reliability
Performance upgrades may affect emissions compliance; confirm ULEZ status
Premium rebuild specifications increase cost but extend longevity under high output
items.cta: Enquire about performance rebuild options for your Bentley Flying Spur

items.question: How do I know which Bentley Flying Spur variant I have?
items.answer: Check your registration document (V5C) for variant designation, or enter your reg above for instant identification. V8 models show 4.0L displacement; W12 shows 6.0L; Hybrid shows combined power output.
keyPoints: V5C registration document lists exact variant and engine code
V8 variants: 4.0L displacement, CTGA/CTGB/CTGC codes
W12 variants: 6.0L displacement, BWB/CCHB/CCHA/BHT codes
Hybrid variants: CHA code with electric motor designation
items.cta: Verify your Bentley Flying Spur variant instantly with registration lookup

items.question: What causes Bentley Flying Spur engine failure most commonly?
items.answer: Most common Flying Spur engine failures: timing chain tensioner wear (W12 BWB/CCHB, V8 CTGA at 70-90k miles), plastic coolant pipe cracks (CCHB W12 at 80-100k miles), and turbo oil seal degradation (all variants at 70k+ miles).
keyPoints: Timing chain tensioner failure causes rattling; upgraded units standard in rebuilds
CCHB W12 plastic coolant pipes crack causing catastrophic overheating
Turbo oil seals wear causing consumption; ceramic cartridges available as upgrade
All documented failures addressed with component upgrades in specialist rebuilds
items.cta: Get quotes for Bentley Flying Spur engines with failure-point upgrades included

items.question: Is a used Bentley Flying Spur engine reliable?
items.answer: Used Flying Spur engines can be reliable if sourced from low-mileage, full-service-history vehicles. All used units in our network undergo compression testing, leak-down checks and DTC scans pre-dispatch.
keyPoints: Compression and leak-down testing essential for used engine validation
Service history verification critical for W12 and Hybrid variants
Used units typically carry 3-6 month warranty vs 12 months on rebuilt
Rebuilt engines offer better long-term value with upgraded failure-point components
items.cta: Compare used vs rebuilt Bentley Flying Spur engine options and warranties

items.question: Do you offer supply and fit for Bentley Flying Spur engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, our network of Bentley-specialist garages offers supply & fit packages nationwide. Book online or receive local garage recommendations with your quote, including ODIS coding and post-install verification.
keyPoints: 40+ Bentley-experienced specialist garages across UK regions
Flexible scheduling to minimise downtime; collection/delivery options available
Supply & fit includes ODIS coding, calibration verification and road testing
W12 and Hybrid variants require specialist certification; confirm garage capability
items.cta: Book Bentley Flying Spur engine supply & fit with local specialist recommendation
